raw:
{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.4": 3, "R1.7": 2, "RX.1": 4, "RX.3": 1, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 3},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The output mentions CMIP6 as a sanity check and briefly compares to uniform sampling but doesn't delve into the broader landscape of related work in climate modeling or surrogate-based approaches.",
    "R1.7": "While the text cites Taylor et al., 2012, it lacks engagement with other relevant literature like CMIP6, IPCC AR6, and potential prior work on cost-aware climate forecasting.",
    "RX.1": "The output acknowledges uncertainty by stating that the surrogate can be wrong by more than 0.08°C, but it doesn't explicitly quantify or analyze the confidence levels associated with different predictions.",
    "RX.3": "The evaluation set is described as being drawn from the 2024 U.S. National Climate Assessment, but there's no indication of whether this set accurately reflects the full scope of potential queries relevant to municipal planners.",
    "RX.4": "The output doesn't identify any harmful patterns in the original data or introduce new errors, fabrications, or harmful content.",
    "RX.5": "While the text mentions training and evaluation against CMIP5, it lacks detailed information about how specific changes were made to the surrogate model and how these changes were traced back to the source material."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.5,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The output mentions CMIP6 as a sanity check and briefly compares to uniform sampling but doesn't delve into the broader landscape of related work in climate modeling or surrogate-based approaches.
rationale.R1.7: While the text cites Taylor et al., 2012, it lacks engagement with other relevant literature like CMIP6, IPCC AR6, and potential prior work on cost-aware climate forecasting.
rationale.RX.1: The output acknowledges uncertainty by stating that the surrogate can be wrong by more than 0.08°C, but it doesn't explicitly quantify or analyze the confidence levels associated with different predictions.
rationale.RX.3: The evaluation set is described as being drawn from the 2024 U.S. National Climate Assessment, but there's no indication of whether this set accurately reflects the full scope of potential queries relevant to municipal planners.
rationale.RX.4: The output doesn't identify any harmful patterns in the original data or introduce new errors, fabrications, or harmful content.
rationale.RX.5: While the text mentions training and evaluation against CMIP5, it lacks detailed information about how specific changes were made to the surrogate model and how these changes were traced back to the source material.
